ety: rel. n. fr. A. sakan, q.v.
def: adj. Habitable.
s.m. The skink, Lacerta scincus.
more: (or siḳanḳūr ) s. m. A skink. (Lacerta scincus).

def: adj. Having thorns, thorny, prickly; troublesome, perilous; — s.m. The aquatic plant Vallisneria.

ety: v.n. fr. سقر 'to burn or scorch'
def: s.m. Hell
more: (from سقر The sunʼs heat burning the face) s. m. Hell.

def: s.m. = sa-kāl, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
more: n. S. स्वीकारक Acceptance or endorsement of a bill.
s. m. Dawn of day, morning.

def: s.m. Acceptance (of a hunḍī or bill), endorsement (of a bill) noting acceptance.
adj. = sakal, q.v. s.m. Early morning, the dawn; — adj. & adv. Seasonable; — seasonably; betimes, early in the morning, at dawn, tomorrow.
adj. Possessed of form or shape, of definite figure, figurate.
Charge for accepting and cashing (a bill of exchange).
more: n. m. The fee charged for acceptance of a bill of exchange.

def: Charge for accepting and cashing (a bill of exchange).
adj. Having some cause, originating from a cause.

ety: pl. of sakrat, v.n. fr. سکر; see sukr
def: s.f. Fainting, insensibility, agony (of death)
more: s. f. Agony, fainting. سکرات موت sakarāt-i-maut, s. f. Senselessness, the point of death, the pangs or agony of death.
subs: s.f. Insensibility attendant upon death, pangs or agony of death.

def: s.m. Great-grandfather, or great-great-grandfather (on the father's side).
adv. = sakāle, q.v. adv. Betimes, early in the morning, at dawn.
s.f. = sakālī or sa-kāl; — adv. = sakāle; qq.v. s.f. Early morning, dawn ( = sakāl); — adv. Early in the morning ( = sakāle).
more: s. f. Dawn of day, morning.

def: adj. (in Gram.), Having an object, transitive.
more: n. m. Opp. of اکرمک S. स with, कर्म्म object. ( Gram .) A verb transitive.

def: v.t. To accept, or endorse as accepted (a bill)
more: v. a. To endorse, accept, or honor a bill. sakāre pīchhe bechnā , v. a. To negotiate a bill after acceptance. sakārne-wālā , n. m. The acceptor of a bill; endorser.
v. a. To accept (a bill of exchange, &c.).
subs: s.m. Accepter, or endorser (of a bill)

ety: sakaṛ˚, &c. = Prk. संकड्ढ(इ), fr. संकड्ढ = S. सङ्क्टष्ट p.p.p. of rt. सम्+कृष्
def: v.n. To draw together, draw in or up, to become contracted, to contract, to become tightened or tight; to dwindle; to shrink, shrivel, to curl up, to wrinkle; to be gathered together, be collected, to gather, collect; to be swept up or together
more: v. n. 1. To be shrunk or contracted, to shrink. 2. To draw in, to collect, gather up, constrain, shrivel, dwindle.
subs: or sikaṛ-jānā, &c. v.n. Intens. of and = sikuṛnā.

def: s.m. Great-grandfather, or great-great-grandfather (on the mother's side).
adj. Having pity, commiserating, pitiful, tender, compassionate.

ety: v.n. fr. سقط 'to fall or drop,' &c.
def: s.m. What is dropped, or thrown away; anything worthless or useless, or held in mean estimation; refuse, rubbish; merchandise of a perishable quality (either spoilt, or spoiling); — an error, blunder, mistake, defect; anything disgraceful or shameful; improper or evil speech, abusive language, abuse
more: (Heb. To rest) s. m. 1. An errour, blunder, mistake, defect. 2. Any thing of a base or useless nature, merchandise of a perishable quality, either spoilt, or spoiling. ¶ saḳt̤, 1. Fire. 2. Sparks struck from a steel. سقط هونا saḳat̤ honā, 1. To die (applied to animals). 2. To miscarry.
subs: v.n. To drop, to die (applied to animals); to be dropped (as the young of an animal), to miscarry.

def: s.f. Power, strength, ability, &c. (see śakti)
s.m. A cart (cf. ćhakṛā).
more: s. m. 1. ( शकट ) A cart. 2. ( संकट ) The name of a Hindū holyday.

def: s.m. A strait, difficulty, pressing distress; — a Hindū festival, the festival of the birthday of Ganeś
subs: s.f. The festival of the birthday of Ganeś.

def: adj. Bad, vile; — s.m. The small tree Trophis aspera.

s.m. A cart ( = ćhakṛā); a cart-load; an implement for preparing grain; a form of military array resembling a wedge; the figure of a cart formed by the five stars composing the lunar asterism Rohiṇī (the fourth mansion of the Hindū zodiac); — śakaṭāsur (˚ṭa+as˚) or śakaṭ, s.m. Name of a daitya or demon slain by the infant Krishṇ.
adj. corr. of شکت saḵẖt, q.v. s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.
more: n. f. S. शक्ती Power; strength ( bal , 7). sakat-dār, sakat-mān , adj. Strong ( bal-vān ).
n. m. Difficulty ( saṅkaṭ ). sakaṭ-chauth, sakaṭ , n. f. A Hin. festival, the birthday of Ganesh .

s. f. 1. Power, strength, ability. 2. sakti, The energy or active power of a deity, personified as his wife, as Gaurī of Siva, Laksh̤mī of Vish̤ṇu. 3. The female organ as the counterpart of the phallick personification of Siva, and worshipped either literally or figuratively by a sect of Hindūs, thence termed saktas. (Wilson).

def: s.f. A small cart; a child's cart, a toy-cart.
s.f. A small cart; a child's cart, a toy-cart.
s.m. A cold bath, a cistern for cold water for washing or bathing, a bath-room.
s.m. A cold bath, a cistern for cold water for washing or bathing, a bath-room.
more: n. m. from سقي Cor. of siqāyā . A cistern for water for a bath.
(prop. siḳāya from سقي) s. m. A cold bath or place for washing the body with cold water in.

def: s.m. A kind of iron; — an iron instrument or weapon; a sword.
more: n. m.S. शङ्कला. A sword ( talvār ).
s. m. A kind of iron.

def: v.n. & t. = sakeṛnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To shrink, to shrivel, to gather up (the limbs, as a tortoise).

def: s.f. A drawing together, contraction, shrinking, &c. ( = sikoṛ).

ety: sakeṛ˚ = S. सङ्क्टष्ट, p.p.p. of सम्+कृष
def: v.n. = sukaṛnā or sikuṛnā, q.v.; — v.t. = sakoṛnā or sikoṛnā, q.v.
v.n. & t. = sakelnā, q.v.
more: v. a.S. सङ्कुलम. See ikhaṭṭāk . (1, 2.). 1. To collect. 2. To sweep ( buhārnā ). Hullā, beṭā! je kuṛā sakeri-ke ghure ḍārī ābo! Hulla, my son! collect the rubbish and throw it on the dunghill!
v. a. See bhīchnā , (3). 1. To shrink; shrivel; collapse. 2. To draw up (the limbs). 3. To compress; contract; narrow; draw in; purse up; pinch; squeeze.

def: adj. Narrow, strait, tight, small; — s.f. Straits, difficulty, distress, want
more: 1. adj. Narrow, strait. 2. s. f. Want, distress.
subs: v.t. To confine, to narrow, &c. ( = saketnā).

def: s.f. = saket, q.v. adj. Narrow, strait, tight, small; — s.f. Straits, difficulty, distress, want
subs: v.t. To confine, to narrow, &c. ( = saketnā).

def: v.t. To draw together, to narrow, confine, to brace up, tighten.
more: v. a. To tighten, to straiten.

ety: inf. n. of سقف 'to make a roof,' &c.
def: s.f. Roof, ceiling; canopy; — the sky; — a platform.
more: s. f. 1. Roof, ceiling, canopy. 2. The sky. 3. A platform.

def: s.f. Beam, rafter.
more: s. f. A beam, a rafter.

def: s.f.m. Evidence (of a witness), testimony; credibility; credit, trust, mercantile credit; name, reputation, honourable character.
s.m. Friend, dear friend ( = sakhi); — the tree Mimosa catechu
subs: s.m. The friend referred to, the aforesaid friend.

def: n. f. S. शाखा sub-division. 1. Season. 2. Harvest; crop.
more: n. f. S. साक्ष्य 1. The evidence of a witness. 2. Weight or credibility; authority. 2. Trust ( etebār , 1); mercantile or commercial credit. 4. Name; reputation; good report; credit. 1. Jisko āṅkh nahīṅ, usko sākh . Prov. No eyes have they to see, yet in their testimony all agree. 2. Kākā na rakkhe sākhā . Prov. Your old slave as your uncle fear, or else your reputation he 'll not spare. 3. Le-ke de chāhat nahīṅ, kiryā karat haiṅ lākh! Tulsī, aise jīv kī kahā kare koī sākh? Tulsī Dās. They take but like not to give back, a lakh of oaths they take, Tulsī! with such as these who e'er can bond or contract make? 4. Jag partīt biṭhāiye, rahiye sāṅche hoë! Jhūṭe nar kī sāṅch hī sākh na māne koë . Your credit in the world established firm, e'er truthful be! The false man's attestation will by no one trusted be. 5. Ijjat to nā bigaṛsī, dil meṅ dhīrī rākh! Jo yahāṅ se uṭh jāëgā bigaṛ jāëgī sākh .…

def: s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: s.m. 1˚ = sākā, q.v.; — 2˚ Quarrel, fight
subs: adj. & s.m. (colloq.) Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Credible, trustworthy; creditable; of good reputation, honourable, respectable.

ety: inf. n. of سخو or سخو 'to be liberal'
def: s.f. = next, q.v.

s.f. A branch, bough; an arm; a party, sect; a division, section, subdivision; a subdivision of the Vedas according to the different schools and redactions of the holy writings; — any part of an animal devoid of sensibility (as a horn, &c.)
subs: (˚khā+uć˚), s.m. The relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: n. m. S. शाक era. 1. Fight ( rāṛ , 1—3.). 1. Sagar gām māṅ dhūm paṛegī, dekh merā terā sākhā . Rus. The village all aroused will be, a fight there 'll be 'twixt you and me. 1. Terā neg dīyā sab manne, phir bhī tauṅ ropā sākhā . Rus. I've given you all your presents due and yet you wrangle and fight with me! 2. An epic; a ballad. 3. An epoch; era, esp. that of Sāli-vāhan commencing from A. D. 78. 4. (S. शाखा, H. sākhā-chār ) The genealogies of the bride and bridegroom's families recited at a Hin. marriage. sākhā ḍālnā yā k. , v. a. To quarrel; fight. sākhe-khor , Wom. n. m. A quarrelsome person.
s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
more: s. f. A branch. (Pers. شاخ shāḵẖ.
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.

def: s.m. Friend, associate, companion
more: n. m. S. सखा A friend; companion.
s. m. A friend, a companion. ¶ sikhā ( शिखा ) s. f. 1. A lock of hair on the crown of the head. 2. Flame.
subs: s.m. pl. A number or host of companions.

def: s.m. corr. of شخض shaḵẖṣ, q.v. s.m. A man's self or person; a person, being, body, individual
subs: s.m. The person in possession, the possessor
s.m. A single person, an individual; an unassisted person.

ety: for A. سخاوة, inf. n. of سخو
def: s.f. Liberality, generosity, munificence.
s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
more: s. m. An evidence, a witness, an eyewitness.

def: s.m. (f.?), A tree.
s.m. (f. -ī) = sakhā, q.v. s.f. A branch; genealogy (in these senses, com. sākhā); a season, time; crop, harvest; — sākhā-mrig, s.m. lit. 'Branch animal'; a monkey; a squirrel
more: n. f. Pr. sahī . 1. A woman's female friend, companion, or confidant. 2. A class of faqīrs who dress like women. 3. An effeminate person. sakhī dauṛ , n. m. A kind of verse in the beginning of a Lāonī , q. v.
s. f. 1. A womanʼs female friend or confidant. 2. A kind of faḳīr, who dresses like a woman.
subs: (sākhā+uć˚), s.m. Relating a genealogy, stating a pedigree.
wety: First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi سکھی (sakhī), borrowed from Sanskrit सखी (sakhī).

def: adj. & s.m. Having branches, branched, branching, ramifying, &c., belonging to any branch, or school, or sect, &c.; — a tree; a Veda (as having various branches or schools); — one who practises the injunctions of any particular branch of the Vedas, a follower of any school or sect.
adj. & s.m. Liberal, bountiful, generous; — a liberal or bountiful person.
more: adj. Liberal; bountiful; munificent. sakhī , n. m. A liberal or bountiful person. Sakhī kā sar buland, mūzī kī gor taṅg . Prov. The liberal man his head bears high, the miser in his narrow grave doth lie.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سخی (saxī), from Arabic سَخِيّ (saḵiyy).

def: s.f. A woman's friend or companion, a female friend or confidante; a handmaid, an abigail; — a class of mendicants who dress like women; an individual of that class; an effeminate man
subs: s.f. A female friend or confidante
s.m. A kind of verse in the beginning of a lāʼonī, q.v.
wety: First attested in c. 1625 as Middle Hindi سکھی (sakhī), borrowed from Sanskrit सखी (sakhī).

def: corr. of सखि sakhi, q.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s.f. = S سکهتو सखित्व sakhitva, s.m. Friendship, intimacy.
s.m. = S سکهتا सखिता sakhitā, s.f. Friendship, intimacy.
s.f. = sakhī, q.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s.m. = sakrā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Bad, impure, polluted (food); — impure food (cf. S. sakaṭānna); unfried food (regarded as not proper to be eaten outside of the rasoʼī, q.v.).

ety: v.n. of sāḵẖtan; sāḵẖ = Zend śać = S. शक्; tan = S. aff. तं
def: s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.
more: (from ساختن) s. f. Make, making, fabrication, fashion, formation, construction, pretence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ساخْت.

ety: prob. Zend śakhta, rt. śac = S. शक्त, rt. शक्; but said to be another form of sanjīda, q.v.
def: adj. Hard, stiff, rigid, firm, fast; strong, solid; tight; constipated; obdurate, obstinate; stingy, sordid; wretched; difficult, arduous, troublesome; painful, grievous; severe, intense, vehement, violent; great, gross, excessive, extreme, egregious; austere, stern, harsh; very cruel, fell; — adv. Very, intensely, violently, severely, excessively, extremely, &c.
more: adj. 1. Hardy; flinty; adamantine; inflexible. Zamīn sakht aur āsmān dūr hai . Prov. The earth so hard, the sky so far. (Whither shall I fly?) 2. Difficult ( bhārī , 12, 13). Sakht kām hai . 3. Obdurate; stubborn; inexorable. 4. Hard-hearted; cruel; unfecling. Beṭā! ab tū mujh se sakhat ho gayā . Wom. 5. Rigid; strict; austere. Woh nambar dene meṅ baṛā sakht hai . 6. Severe; violent; grievous; harsh. 7. Great; gross ( bhārī , 19). 8. Stingy; close-fisted ( kanjūs ). 9. Inauspicious; unlucky. Sakht din. sakht , adv. Severely; violently; extremely, etc. sakht be-insāfī , Great injustice. sakht be-īmānī , Gross breach of trust. sakht zamīn , n. f. 1. Hard soil or ground. 2. Firm ground. sakht sust kahnā , v. a. To use harsh words; rail at ( āṛe hāth lenā , 2, 5.).

adj. 1. Hard, strong. 2. Difficult. 3. Obdurate. 4. Stingy. 5. Wretched, troublesome, violent, vehement, very cruel, fell, painful, austere, rigid, severe, harsh (to the ear). 6. adv. Very, intensely, violently, extremely. سخت انداز saḵẖt-andāz, An archer. سخت گوي saḵẖt-go-ī, s. f. Harshness of language, abuse. سخت گير saḵẖt-gīr, Holding fast (an animal of chase). سخت گيري saḵẖt-gīr-ī, s. f. Extortion, taking by force.
subs: adj. Of austere countenance, stern-faced, sour-looking
(-se), To reproach, scold, chide, to rail (at)
adj. & s.m. Harsh or unpleasant of speech; saying unpleasant things; — one who speaks harshly or says unpleasant things
s.f. Harshness or unpleasantness of language; abuse
adj. Holding fast (an animal of chase)
s.f. Extortion, exaction, taking by force.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سخت (saxt), from Middle Persian 𐭮𐭠𐭤𐭨 (saxt), 𐭮𐭤𐭨 (saxt).

ety: perf. part. of saḵẖtan; see sāḵẖt
def: part. adj. Made, formed; artificial, counterfeited, fictitious, false, feigned; surreptitious
more: part. adj. 1. Artificial; not natural (جعلي). 2. Surreptitious. sākhtah pardākhtah , n. m. lit . Done and executed. Deed; act. sākhtah pardākhtah ek kā misl sākhtah pardākhtah dūsre ke hai . ( Law .) The act of one shall be held as the act of the other. be-sākhtah , adj. 1. Unaffected; not forced; natural. 2. Simple ( sādah , 2, 3.) Ajab ek ālam thā be-sākhtah, Ki ālam thā ek us pe jāṅ-bākhtah . Mīr Hasan. A world of beauty strange upsprung, A world devoted 'bout her hung. 3. Unpremeditated; unprepared; impromptu; extempore . be-sākhtah , adv. 1. Without preparation; spontaneously. 2. See dafatan .
part. pass. (from ساختن) Made, formed; artificial, fictitious, stiff.
subs: and sāḵẖta-pardākhta, part. adj. & s.m. Done and effected; anything done or had done, act, deed
adj. & adv. Natural, unprepared, unaffected; simple; unpremeditated, impromptu, extempore; without preparation, spontaneously, on the spur of the moment.

def: s.f. Make, &c. ( = sāḵẖt, q.v.); art, design; effort; machination. s.f. Making, make, construction, structure, formation, manufacture, fabrication; form, fashion, figure, shape, mould; — pretence; hypocrisy.

s.f. Hardness, stiffness, rigidity, firmness; tightness; stinginess; obduracy, obstinacy; intenseness, intensity, vehemence, severity; harshness, asperity; sternness, austereness; violence, atrocity; cruelty; grievance, hardship; adversity, indigence, distress, difficulty, evil, calamity
more: n. f. 1. Hardness; firmness; rigidity; inflexibility. Iske peṭ meṅ baṛī sakhtī hai . 2. Obduracy; stubbornness. 3. Hard-heartedness; cruelty. 4. Harshness; severity; asperity. 5. Violence ( tāddī , 1—4). 6. Strictness; rigidity; austerity. 7. Poverty; indigence. 8. Distress ( taṅgī , 3). sakhtī se , adv. Harshly; severely, etc. sakhtī se pesh ānā , v. a. 1. To treat harshly. 2. To be strict or rigorous. sakhtī k. , v. a. To use force or violence, etc.

s. f. 1. Hardness, firmness, harshness, intenseness, vehemence, severity, asperity, atrocity, grievance, hardship. 2. Obduracy. 3. Stinginess, indigence. 4. Distress, difficulty. سختي کوپينا saḵẖtī ko pīnā, (dakh.) See برداشت کرنا bar-dāsht karnā under برداشت.
subs: (-se), To use force or violence (towards), treat with harshness or severity, &c.

def: s.m. = sāgūn and sāṅkhū, qq.v. s.m. Omen, augury (cf. shugūn).
s.m. Friendship, intimacy; equality.

adv. &c. = sākshāt, q.v. adv. Before the eyes, in sight (of), in the presence (of), before; eye to eye, face to face; visibly, evidently, openly, manifestly; as, like, like as; — adj. Visible, evident, open, manifest, conspicuous; precisely like, exact, true, identical, the very same; — s.m. Interview, meeting
more: 1. adj. Evident, conspicuous. 2. adv. Before, in the presence of.
subs: adj. Causing to be visibly present, making evident, proving.

def: s.m. 1˚ = sākhī or sākshī; — 2˚ = sākhī or śākhī; qq.v. s.m. = sākshī; — 2˚ s.f. = sākshī and sākshya; — 3˚ s.f. = sakhī; qq.v.
s. f. (dakh.) See سکهي sakhī. ¶ sukī, See سکهي sukhī.

ety: for A. ساقٍ sāqiṉ, act. part. of سقي 'to give (water, &c.
def: s.m. One who gives to drink, cup-bearer, page; (in India) one who gets ready and brings the ḥuqqa
more: Poet. n. m. سقي gave him to drink. 1. A cup-bearer; a cup-boy. Take hai zāhid sharāb-i-gulgūṅ, huā hai dil bhī kharāb ādhā, Khilā de, sāqī, balā se isko ḍabo-ke tū bhī kabāb ādhā! Saiyad. The saint stares at the wine, his heart with foulness is half-choked, Give him, ne'er mind, cup boy! just half the roast in wine full soaked. 2. A sweetheart; one beloved; love. Mai bhī hai, mīnā bhī hai, sāgar bhī hai, sāqī nahīṅ! Jī meṅ ātā hai lagā deṅ āg mai-khāne ko ham . Goyā. Wine too is here, wine-cup and flask, the cup-bearer is not! I could this public house with fire destroy and bring to naught. sāqiyā! O cup bearer! O cup boy!
(part. act. of سقي Heb. To irrigate) s. m. A cupbearer, a page. (Plur. ساقيان sāḳiyān ). ساقيا sāḳiyā, O cupbearer!
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَاقِی (sāqī), from Arabic سَاقٍ (sāqin).

def: (act. part. of ثقب 'to pierce'), adj. lit. 'Penetrating'; shining brightly, glistening, gleaming very brightly, brilliant, splendid; famous, exalted, sublime, high.
more: (from ثقب) adj. 1. Shining, splendid. 2. Sublime, high.

def: adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous; hard to be borne, onerous, burdensome, oppressive, afflictive; indigestible; slow, sluggish, indolent, lazy, phlegmatic; dull, torpid, inactive.
more: adj. 1. Heavy. 2. Indigestible. Arviyāṅ saqīl hotī haiṅ .
(from ثقل) adj. 1. Heavy. 2. Lazy, phlegmatick. 3. Indigestible.

def: v.t. To bring together, to collect, &c.; — to push away.

r: saqilāt̤, siqlāt̤, suqlāt̤, sijillāt̤, sijlāt̤, saqillāt, saqilāt, saqallāt, saqalāt
def: s.f. (?) Scarlet cloth.

r: saqillāt, saqilāt, saqallāt, saqalāt, saqilāt̤, siqlāt̤, suqlāt̤, sijillāt̤, sijlāt̤
def: s.f. (?) Scarlet cloth.

ety: prob. fr. S. सङ्कीर्ण, rt. कृ with सम्
def: v.n. To gather, collect, accumulate, to convene; to purse; to shrink, &c. ( = sikuṛnā).
adj. Diseased, disordered, sick, ailing; infirm, weak; unsound, faulty, defective.

ety: act. part. of سکن 'to be still, or stationary,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. In a state of rest, quiet, calm, tranquil, peaceable; quiescent (letter); inhabiting, dwelling; — a quiescent letter, a consonant not followed by a vowel; — an inhabitant, resident.
more: n. m. سکن was at rest. 1. An inhabitant; a resident. 2. ( Gram .) A consonant not followed by a vowel; a mute (H. halant ). sākin , adj. 1. Quiescent; at rest. 2. ( Gram .) Mute.
(part. act. of سکن Heb. To rest, to dwell). 1. s. m. An inhabitant (Pers. plur. ساکنان). 2. adj. Quiet, peaceable. 3. Quiescent (letter).

def: s.f. A kind of inferior divinity (or female demon or fairy) attendant chiefly on Śiva and Durgā.
more: s. f. A female divinity of an inferiour character, attendant especially on Siva and Durgā.

ety: act. part. of سقط 'to fall, drop,' &c.
def: part. adj. Falling, dropping; fallen, dropped; unavailable; out of use, obsolete; lost; degraded, depraved, vile, contemptible
more: part. act. سقط fell. Dropped; lost. sāqit k. , v. a. To throw out; cast; reject. sāqit huā . A bated; destroyed. sāqit h. , v. n. To abate; lapse; to be lost. Yahāṅ se matlab sāqit hotā hai .
part. act. (of سقط Heb. To fall, to be quiet) Falling; fallen, vile. ساقط هونا sāḳit̤ honā, To be fallen, to be degraded.
subs: v.t. To drop, to throw down; to cast; to reject
v.n. To drop, fall; to abate. to lapse; to be dropped; to become obsolete; to be fallen, be lost, degraded.

ety: act. part. of سکت 'to be silent'
def: adj. Silent, quiet, mute, at rest.
more: (part. act. of سکت Heb. To be silent) adj. Silent, quiet.

def: part. Capable of being accomplished, capable, able.
s.m. pl. of sāqī, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. سقي 'to give to drink'
def: s.m. Water-carrier; one who gives to drink, a cup-bearer ( = sāqī); waterer (of plants, &c.); — a pelican ( = murg-ě-saqqā, — so called because it draws water for itself).
more: (from سقي Heb. To water, to give to drink) s. m. 1. A water-carrier. 2. A cupbearer.

def: n. m. saqnī , n. f. سقي gave water. 1. A water-carrier ( bhishtī ). 2. The pelican, so called from a tradition that, during a severe drought at Mecca, numbers of these sagacious birds brought water in their pouches for the relief of the inhabitants of the sacred city.

ety: v.n. fr. سكّ 'to make firm'; 'to clamp,' &c.
def: s.m. (orig.) 'A stamper of money'; a maker of knives, a cutler.
more: (from سکّ To cover or strengthen with plates of iron, from Heb. To cover) s. m. A cutler, one who makes knives.

def: s.f. The business or craft of a cutler.
more: s. f. The business of a cutler.

def: s.f. Sugar.
more: A prefix signifying beyond, remote, another, as sakaṛ dādā. sakaṛ-nānā , Maternal great grandfather.

def: s.f. Name of a rāginī; a kind of tune or music.

ety: prob. through Syriac, fr. Gr. Σκαμμωνία
def: s.m. (f. ?) Scammony (dried mucillage from the root of Convolvulus scammonea).
n. f. Scammony.
more: s. m. Scammony. ( Hippoc. and Diosc. σκαμμωνια Dried juice exuding from the root of Convolvulus scammonea).

def: s.m. Inhabitants, people, inmates (of a house), family.

ety: sak˚ = Prk. सक्क(इ) = S. शक्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. शक्
def: v.n. To be able, can (commonly annexed to the base of a verb, or to an inflected infinitive, to form potentials; e.g. khā-saknā, 'to be able to eat'; jāne-saknā, 'to be able to go').
more: v. n. S. शकन from शक be able; Pr. sakkaī he is able. To be able; can.
v. n. To be able. ¶ suknā, v. n. (dakh.) See سوکنا sūknā. ¶ siknā, v. a. (dakh.) To learn: see سيکهنا sīkhnā.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa सक्कइ (sakkaï), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (sakkaï), from Sanskrit श॒क्नोति॑ (śaknóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćaknáwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱek- + *-néwti.

def: s.m. = سکنة saknat, q.v. s.f. A rest, pause (esp. in reading the Qorʼān); the quiescence of a letter, or its being void of a vowel.

v.n. = saknā, q.v. v.n. To be able, can (commonly annexed to the base of a verb, or to an inflected infinitive, to form potentials; e.g. khā-saknā, 'to be able to eat'; jāne-saknā, 'to be able to go').
more: v. n. (dakh.) To fear, to be afraid ( Harris ).

def: s.f. A rest, pause (esp. in reading the Qorʼān); the quiescence of a letter, or its being void of a vowel.

ety: S. सङ्कोचः, rt. कुच् with सम्

v.t. = saṅkoćnā, q.v. v.n. To shrink; — v.t. To cause to shrink; to make one ashamed, to abash.
adj. Enraged, wrathful, angry.
adj. Full of anger, enraged, angry, displeased.
s.m. = sakorā, q.v. s.m. A small earthen cup.
more: n. m. Dim. sakorī ; Sah. kasorī , n. f. A small earthen cup.

def: s.m. A small earthen cup.
more: s. m. A small earthen vessel.

def: s.f. dim. of sakorā, q.v.

ety: caus. of sakaṛnā or sikuṛnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To draw together, to contract, compress, to draw up or in, to brace up, to tighten; to crumple up, &c.; — v.n. = sikuṛnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To shrink, to draw up (the limbs), to gather up, to shrivel, to tighten, contract. v. a. To contract, draw up.

def: s.m. = شکر śakr, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Bad, impure, polluted (food); — impure food (cf. S. sakaṭānna); unfried food (regarded as not proper to be eaten outside of the rasoʼī, q.v.).
more: n. m. See chhūt . Unfried food, held by Hindus not proper to be eaten outside the rasoī , q. v. sak'rā, sakrī , adj. 1. Unfried (food). 2. Touched; polluted ( jhūṭā ).

adj. S. सङ्कीर्ण Narrow (بهيرَا). Ham chauṛe, bazār sakṛā . Prov. I am broad, and the bāzār is narrow. (Said of a great boaster). suk'ṛā , adj. Shrunk; shrivelled; contracted. suk'ṛā konā ; A zāviah hādda . An acute angle.
adj. Small (not having room enough), narrow, strait.

ety: prob. = sakṛā, q.v.; or S. सङ्कट+कः; Prk. संखडओ or संकडओ
def: adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrow, strait, not roomy, small, tight.

r: śakra, śakr, lit., Pentaptera arjuna, Nerium antidysentericum, śakrā-śan, ˚ra, aś˚, Wrightia antidysenterica, śakra-bhū-bhavā
def: s.f. Colocynth, Cucumis coloquintida
subs: s.m. Name of a red insect, the coccinella; the lady-bird.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شکر (šakar, “sugar”).

ety: perf. part. of sakaṛnā, &c.; Prk. संकड्ढओ; S. सङ्क्टष्ट+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Drawn together, contracted, compressed, braced up, tightened, tight; circumscribed; acute (angle); gathered up; shrunk, shrivelled, wrinkled, withered.

ety: sakrā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता + इका
def: s.f. Contractedness, narrowness, straitenedness, want of room, smallness.
s.f. Fee charge for acceptance of a bill of exchange ( = sakārā, q.v.).
s. f. Smallness, want of room, narrowness.

ety: sakrā, 'narrow,' q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To contract, to straiten, to deprive of necessary room (cf. sakṛānā).
more: v. a. 1. ( संकीर्ण ) To straiten, to deprive of necessary room. 2. ( स्वीकारण or caus. of سکارنا) To cause to accept (bill, &c.).

def: v.t. To cause (one) to accept (a bill), to have or get (a bill) accepted.
v.t. To draw together, to contract, to brace, to tighten, &c. (see sukaṛnā); — v.n. = sukaṛnā.
s.f. = saṅkrānt or saṅkrānti, q.v. part. Met together, passed through or into; passed, gone; gone from one point to another, transferred; entered (into a new sign of the zodiac).
more: s. f. The entering of the sun into a new sign.

def: adj. & s.f. See saṅkrāntī.
more: 1. adj. Relating to the سکرانت sakrānt. 2. s. f. See سنکرانتي sankrāntī.

def: adv. Once; at once, together; together with, with; always, ever.
more: adj. S. सुकृति Well-done or performed. sukarit , n. m. Virtue; good actions. sukarit k. , v. n. To do good.

def: adj. Angry, wrathful, enraged.
(sa+ak˚), Having eyes; — having the seeds of which rosaries are made; having a rosary.
s.m. contrac. of sākshya, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Having syllables or letters, containing syllables; — s.m. Orthography.

adv. Before the eyes, in sight (of), in the presence (of), before; eye to eye, face to face; visibly, evidently, openly, manifestly; as, like, like as; — adj. Visible, evident, open, manifest, conspicuous; precisely like, exact, true, identical, the very same; — s.m. Interview, meeting
more: adj. 1. Evident; conspicuous. 2. Exact; true; identical; the very same. sākshāt' , adv. Before; in the presence of; openly.
subs: adj. Causing to be visibly present, making evident, proving.

def: adj. & s.m. Observing, witnessing; attesting, testifying, bearing testimony, giving evidence; — observer, eye-witness, witness (in law)
more: n. f. See اظﮩـار 3. 1. Attestation; sign manual. 2. Testimony. 3. An eye-witness; a witness. sākshī denā yā k . To call to or bear witness. sākshī likh'nā , v. n. To attest; witness. Īshvar ko sākshī denā , Hin.; Khudā ko gavāh k. , Mah. v. a. To call God to witness. pramān sākshī . A subscribing witness.
subs: or sākshī karnā (-ko), To give (one) as a witness, to make (one) a witness, to call (one) to witness.

r: sākshī, sākshya, sākshī denā, sākshī karnā
def: s.f. = S ساکشيه साक्ष्य sākshya, s.m. Testimony, evidence, witness, attestation

r: sākshya, sākshī, sākshī denā, sākshī karnā
def: s.m. = H ساکشي साक्षी sākshī, s.f. Testimony, evidence, witness, attestation
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit साक्ष्य (sākṣya). Doublet of ساکھ (sākh).

def: adj. = شکت śakt, q.v.
s.m. Sparks struck from a steel; fire.
s.f. = شکتی śakti, q.v.
s.m. corr. of شاکت śākt, q.v.
s.m. A worshipper of the divine power under its female representation (as Devī, Durgā, &c.; see śakti).
adj. (f. -ā), Able, capable, competent; strong, mighty, powerful; hard, compact, tight, fast; clever, diligent; attentive, intent; affable, pleasing, civil, polite.

ety: for A. سکتة saktat, v.n. fr. سکت 'to be or become silent'
def: s.m. Apoplexy; a trance, swoon; stupefaction; — a pause, division, vacancy, hiatus.
more: n. m. سکت became still. 1. Stupefaction; a swoon; apoplexy; a trance. Uskī sūrat dekhte hī woh sakte ke ālam meṅ ho gayā . 2. Rough or bad verse. Is sher meṅ saktā hai. saktah paṛnā , v. n. To halt or drag (a verse). saktah h. , v. n. To fall into a swoon.
(from سکت) s. m. A trance, an apoplexy, a pause, vacancy, hiatus.

def: s.f. Ability, capacity, capability, power, strength, hardness.
s.m. The thigh; a bone; — pole or shafts of a cart; frame of a cart.
s.f. Attachment, contact, union, junction, cohesion; addiction (to); devotion (to).

s.f. Ability, power, capacity, capability, faculty, strength, might, main, energy, ascendancy, prowess; the energy or active power of a deity personified as his wife (as Gaurī of Śiva, Lakshmī of Vishṇu, &c.); the female organ (as the counterpart of the phallic representation of Śiva, and worshipped either literally or figuratively by a sect of Hindūs termed Sāktas)
more: n. f. S. शक to be able. 1. See bal , 7. Bin shaktī Shiv shau . 2. Ability; capacity. 3. The energy or active power of a god, personified as his wife, as Gauri of Shiv, Lakshmī of Vishnu . 4. The female organ as the counterpart of the phallic personification of Shiv, worshipped either literally or figuratively by a sect of Hindus , thence termed Shāktī .
subs: śakti-mān, or śakti-mant, adj. (f. -matī), Possessed of ability, powerful, strong, mighty, able; possessing a competence
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शक्ति (śakti)

def: s.f. = śakti, and sakat, qq.v.

s.m. A casualty-list of horses that have died in the cavalry.
more: n. m. 1. ( nāma o payām ). A letter ( chiṭṭhī , 1). 2. ( In Comp .) A writing; written document; deed; as iqrār-nāmah-bar , a deed of agreement. nāmah-bar , n. m. A messenger; letter-carrier. nāma-nigār , A correspondent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نامه (nāma).

def: s.m. A species of poison.

s.f. Shrivelling up, contraction, compression; shrinking, shyness, coyness, bashfulness, shame; fear, dread, awe
more: n. f. S. सङ्कोच from सम with, कुच Shrink. 1. Shrinking; shyness; coyness; bashfulness; modesty. Lāj, sharam, sakuch kī bāt, tum se maiṅ kah na sakūṅ, Pinḍilyāṅ tharrāëṅ, kamar bal khāë, eṛī darad kare . Jachchā rānī. I shrink and blush and cannot tell you what 't were shame to tell, Trembling my legs, my sides contort, a pain. I feel as well. 2. Doubt ( dubdhā , 1).
s. f. Shrinking, awe, fear, shame.

ety: S. सङ्कुचित+कः, or perf. part. of sakućnā
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrowed, compressed, tightened, tight; — shrivelled up, shrunk; closed, bound; abashed, ashamed; intimidated, awe-stricken ( = sakućāyā).
s.f. Shrinking; bashfulness; apprehensiveness; &c. (see sakuć).
v.t. To cause to shrink; to compress, to tighten; to abash; to frighten intimidate, overawe; — v.n. = sakućnā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To shrink; to be shy, bashful, modest. 2. To be frightened; to hesitate.
v. n. To be abashed, to be afraid. v. a. To abash.

def: adj. = sakućāyā; — s.f. = sakućāʼī or sakuć.

part. adj. (f. sakućāʼī) = sakućā, q.v. part. adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrowed, compressed, tightened, tight; — shrivelled up, shrunk; closed, bound; abashed, ashamed; intimidated, awe-stricken ( = sakućāyā).
v.n. To shrink, contract, become narrow or tight; to shrink (from), be afraid (of), be apprehensive (of), to fear; to hesitate; to be abashed, be shy, or bashful.
more: v. n. To fear, to be afraid of, to be in awe of, to be abashed, to apprehend, to be apprehensive.

def: adj. & s.m. Having a family; belonging to a noble family, of good lineage or stock; belonging to the same family; — a family man; — a kinsman; — a kind of fish (cf. saur).

s. (dakh.) Polish, furbishing ( Harris ): see صيقل ṣaiḳal. شکل کرنا shikal karnā, To polish, burnish, furbish. شکل گر shikal-gar, Polisher, armourer, burnisher, furbisher.

more: n. All the relations and connections of whatever degree. sakulya nevtā , n. A general invitation including the whole fraternity.

def: s.m. An omen ( = sagun, shugūn).
s.m. A bird (in general); a kite; an eagle; a vulture; — an auspicious object or mark, a lucky omen, a prognostic, an omen (in general, but rarely applied to anything inauspicious).
more: s. m. Any lucky or auspicious object or omen. See شگون.

s.m. A bird (in general); the Indian kite, Falco cheela; — name of one of the astronomical periods called invariable karaṇs; — name of the Aśvins.

s.m. The saul tree, Shorea robusta, and its wood; — the plane-tree; — a wall surrounding a building, a wall (in general); enclosure, court; — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — the gilt-head fish, Sparus spilotus
more: n. m. S. शाल. 1. (Sant. sarjom ) A common timber tree; Shorea robustā , Wat. 2. (S. शल्य ) Perforation; bore; mortise; a hole made by driving a pin into the ground. 3. (Fr. salle ) A hall; a house; place. 4. A Hindi school. 5. A fish; Ophiocephalus wraht . (?)

n. m. Z. saredha , S. बर्ष A year. sāl-i-āindah , n. m. The next or ensuing year. sāl-i-bāqīdārī . A year of default; a year in which default occurs. sāl ba-sāl , adv. Year by year ( sālāna ). sāl bhar , adv. All the year round. sāl-i-paivastah ; H. tevras , The year before last. sāl tamām, sāl tamāmi , An annual report. sāl tamām par , At the end of the year. sāl jhaṛtī jama kharch . The official or financial year. sāl-khurdā , adj. 1. Old; used up; worn out. 2. Experienced. sāl-i-zirāat , Ped. The agricultural year. sāl-i-faslī . The faslī year.

s. m. 1. ( साल ) A common timber tree. (Shorea robusta: Roxb. Pl. Cor. ). 2. ( शल्य ) A thorn. 3. Perforation, bore, mortise, a hole made by driving a pin into the ground, &c. 4. ( शाला ) s. f. House, place. 5. A school. 6. (for श्रृगाल ) s. m. A jackal.

s. m. A year. سال بسال sāl ba-sāl, Year by year, annually. سال حاصل sāl-ḥāṣil, Yearly produce. سال خورده sāl-ḵẖụrda, adj. Old, in years. سالگره sāl-girih, s. f. subs: or śāl-bhanjī, s.f. A doll, a wooden puppet; a kind of game played in the east of India
s.f. lit. 'Having śāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شال (šāl). Doublet of سَاڑِی (sāṛī). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਾਲ (śāl) / شال (śāl), Gujarati શાલ (śāl), Marathi शाल (śāl), Kannada ಶಾಲು (śālu), Telugu శాలువ (śāluva).

def: s.m. A porcupine's quill; — a dart, spear: — a kind of field; — an epithet of Brahmā.
more: (Heb. To plunder, to spoil) adj. One who can take nothing in his hands (from sickness), paralytick, having the hand luxated or withered.

def: v. a. 1. To prepare one's own funeral pyre; to become a satī , q. v. Tiryā tujh meṅ tīn gun, aur augan haiṅ lakh chār, Mangal gāve, sal rache, aur kokan upjeṅ lāl . Three virtues, woman, yours, in faults four hundred thousand set, You benisons sing, self immolate, and children you beget. 2. To manage the house. sal se baiṭhnā , v. n. To be settled or composed.
s.f. House, place (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dharm-sālā, pāṭ-sālā, qq.v., and cf. sāl).
more: n. m. S. श्याल 1. A wife's brother. 1. Jitne kāle, utne mere bāp ke sāle . Prov. As many blacks so many brothers-in-law my father has had. 2. Rāvan kā sālā . Prov. The brother-in-law of the great Rāvan. (Said of one who tyrannizes under the protection of a powerful person). 2. A common term of abuse implying the disgrace attaching to a man whose sister has been had by another; hence, a pander; pimp. Ranḍī māṅge rupaiyā—'Le le, merī Maiyā, Phakkaṛ māṅge paisā—'Chal be, sāle, kaisā ?' Prov. The harlot money doth demand—'Take take it, mammy dear, it's here,' The beggar wants a pice—'Out pander of thy sister's honor dear!' jagat-sālā , n. m. 1. A brother of a harlot. 2. An abuse.
s. m. 1. (for श्याल ) A wifeʼs brother, brother-in-law. 2. ( शाला in compos. mostly) House, place.

def: s.m. Wife's brother, brother-in-law; a term of abuse.
s.f. A large branch of a tree; — a hall, room, house, place; a school, a stable ( = sālā).
s.f. Calling or inviting (beggars, &c.), to receive or partake of food; invitation; annunciation; proclamation; voice, call, cry (of an auctioneer, or salesman, &c.); shout, challenge (of a foe).
more: n. m. An invitation. Salā na shud, balā shud . P. Prov. No feast of pleasure, of ills a measure.

def: (fr. sāl), adj. aff. Years old, &c. (used with numerals, e.g. du-sāla, 'Two years old'; ṣad-sāla, 'of a hundred years').
more: ( In comp .) A termination affixed to numbers, as do-sālah , two year old.
(in compos.) A termination affixed to numbers, to denote so many years old; as دوساله do-sāla, Two years old; پنجساله panj-sāla, Five years old; صد ساله ṣad-sāla, Of a hundred years.

def: s.m. A fox
subs: s.f. Salep, the root of a species of orchis, Orchis mascula (considered, particularly in the East, to be a strong restorative and aphrodisiac).

def: s.m. A (hard, rough) stone or slab for grinding drugs, &c. on (syn. kharal).

ety: for A. صلابة, inf. n. of صلب 'to be hard,' &c.
def: s.f. Firmness, hardness, stiffness, rigidity; severity, rigour; strength; valour; majesty, dignity, awe.
more: (from صلب) s. f. Firmness, hardness, severity, majesty, dignity.

def: s. (dakh.) An intrenchment ( Harris ).
s.m. = شلبهہ śalabh, q.v.
s.m. A grasshopper; a locust.

ety: inf. n. of سلف 'to pass away,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Past, preceding, former (times, &c.); — such as have gone before, predecessors, ancestors; past or former times; — money advanced for merchandise, &c.; — a purse, bag.
more: n. f. سلف passed away. Past or old times. Salaf se hotī āī hai .

(from سلف To level the ground, to go before) 1. adj. Past, preceding, former (times). 2. s. m. Ancestors. 3. Money advanced for merchandise, &c. 4. A purse, a bag. 5. The past, former times. ¶ silf, Brother-in-law, husband of a wifeʼs sister.
s. (dakh.) A barge, a bark ( Harris ).

def: adj. Joined together, in one piece, unbroken, continuous, uninterrupted; — s.m. (local), Close friendship, intimacy, familiarity.
more: adj. Unbroken; uninterrupted; continuous. Ek salag paṭṭī utār lo .

def: v. n. (dakh.) See سلگنا sulagnā.

s.m. A species of black quartzose (found in the river Gandhak), containing the impression of one or more ammonites (conceived by the Hindūs to represent Vishṇu; and hence it is worshipped by the Vaishṇavas).
more: n. m. S. शालग्राम An ovoid black quartzose worn by the action of water and sometimes found impregnated with gold. It bears the impression of one or more ammonites, conceived by the Hindus to represent Vishnū who was turned to stone by a curse of Binda, wife of the Rakshas or demon Jalandar. Kūṛh nār se murgī bhalī jo ande deve bīs, Sālagrām se chakkī bhalī jo āṭā deve pīs . Kabīr. For a stupid wife give me a hen that twenty eggs will lay, For holy stone give me a mill to grind my corn each day.
s. m. A flinty stone, containing the impression of one or more ammonites, conceived by the Hindūs to represent Vish̤ṇu.

ety: inf. n. of صلح 'to be good, right, just, &c.
def: s.f. Goodness (of character, or of state or condition); well-being, prosperity; rectitude, virtue, probity, honesty; right or proper course (of conduct or procedure); good and proper thing, right thing, prudent measure; advisableness, propriety, fitness; advice, counsel; consultation; — (quasi-inf. n. of اصلح, iv of صلح), rectification, adjustment, agreement, harmony, concord, reconciliation, peace, treaty ( = iṣlāḥ, q.v.); — adj. Good, right, proper, suitable, favourable, advisable, prudent
more: n. f. صلح was right. 1. Counsel (اپديش 1, 3, & islāh ). Nek salāh kā pūchhnā kyā? Prov. Saying. No one refuses a good thing. 2. Choice; volition ( irādah , 1-4). Ṭhairī hai unke āne kī yān kal pe jā salāh, Aë jān-i-barlab-āmdah, hai is meṅ terī kyā salāh! Zauq. To-morrow she 'll here, 'tis settled now at last, What say you to it, Life! from me now ebbing fast? salāh par chalnā , v. a. To act according to advice. salāh denā , v. a. To advise; counsel. salāh se , adv. In communication with; with the advice of. salāh-kār, salāh dene-vālā , n. m. An adviser; a counsellor. salāh-kār-i-ryāsat ; H. rāj-mantrī , n. m. A counsellor of State. salāh k., yā lenā , v. a. To consult; advise with; take counsel. salāh nahīṅ . Not advisable; inexpedient. salāh-i-vaqt , adj. Advisable. salāh o mashvarah , n. m. Advice and counsel. be-salāh k. , v. a. To act without advice. pakkī salāh . 1. Sound policy. 2…

(from صلح Heb. To prosper) 1. s. f. Peace, concord, treaty. 2. Rectitude, integrity, goodness of state or condition, prosperity. 3. Advice, counsel. 4. adj. Advisable. صلاح کرني ṣalāḥ karnī, To consult, advise, to take counsel together.
subs: (kisi-kī), To act according to the advice (of)
(-ko), To give advice (to), to advise, counsel
(-se), To hold consultation (with), to advise (with), to consult
(-kī), To take the advice (of); to consult
Is not advisable; is inexpedient; is not a prudent course
s.f. A fit or proper opportunity, a favourable time; — the right course for the time or occasion; — adj. Advisable.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic صَلَاح (ṣalāḥ).

ety: for A. صلاحية, inf. n. of صلح 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Goodness, rectitude, integrity, probity, honesty; virtue, chastity; sanctity; — welfare, well-being, prosperity; — mildness, gentleness; — ability, capability, fitness, worth; — a deposition; a report or return (made by the police) on the state of a district
more: n. f. 1. Capacity; capability. 2. Mildness; gentleness. 3. A deposition ( izhār , 3). 4. A report or return made by the police. salāhiyāt-bahī , n. f. A diary kept in police and revenue offices. salāhiyat likhnā , v. n. To keep a register of travellers in a road-side inn.
(from صلح) s. f. Virtue, chastity, integrity, goodness, probity, sanctity.
subs: s.f. A diary (kept in police and revenue offices) of the condition or prosperity of a district
(-kī), To make a (police) report on the state of a district; to keep a register of travellers in a sarāʼc, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَلَاحِیَّت (salāhiyyat), from Arabic صَلَاحِيَّة (ṣalāḥiyya). By surface analysis, صَلاح (salāh) + ـِیَت (-iyat).

def: (from سلاح Armour, and شور Exercise, habitude) adj. A champion, gladiator, horse-breaker.

s.f. A piece of wire, a needle, a probe, any pointed surgical instrument; a catheter; a bodkin; a large needle used for tinging the eyes with collyrium; a piece of wire, or needle, &c. used to deprive a person of sight (by drawing it, while hot, over the eyes); a lead-pencil; a brimstone match; a line, streak, stripe
more: n. f. S. शलाका A. salābah . 1. Any thing in the form of a needle. 2. A large needle used for tinging the eyelids with a collyrium. 3. A large needle, probe, or wire heated and drawn across the eyes so as to cause loss of sight. 4. A bodkin. 5. A catheter. 6. (Fac.) Membrum virile . 7. Matches ( dīyā-salāī ). 8. A line; stripe. salāī phernā , v. a. To blind; deprive of sight. salāī-dār , adj. Striped or twilled (cloth).
subs: (-par), To pass a red-hot wire or needle, &c. (across the eyes of a person), to deprive of sight, to blind

def: s.f. Brother-in-law's wife ( = sarhaj).
more: Māṛ. n. f. S. श्याल जाया Brother-in-law's wife.

def: s.f. = sarhaj and salaij, qq.v. s.f. (dialec.), Wife's brother's wife (cf. sāṛhū).
n. f. S. शिलाजतु 1. Storax; stone lac; mineral coal; bitumen; red chalk; benzoin. 2. A beautiful crystallized foliated gypsum.
adj. (f. -ā), Feeling shame or modesty, bashful, modest, ashamed.
more: ( स With, and लज्जा Shame) adj. Shameful, ashamed.

def: adj. Painful, afflictive, troublous.
s.f. (local) = salāḵẖ, q.v.; and cf. next. s.f. A bar (of iron); an ingot (of gold or silver); a stake; a spit; a probe; — a continuous line, a streak.
s.f. A probe; a rule, ruler, &c. (see śalākā); a line, streak.
more: s. f. 1. A rule, a ruler. 2. A probe.

def: s.f. A dart, a javelin; an arrow; a rib (of an umbrella, &c.); a thin bar (of a cage, &c.); a small stake, stick, rod, peg, pin; a round pointed piece of wood or bamboo (serving for various purposes); a rule, ruler; a brimstone-match; any pointed surgical instrument, a tent or probe; a bone; an oblong quadrangular piece of ivory or bone (used in particular games), a domino; a fibrous stick (used as a brush, &c.); a tooth-brush; a toothpick; a pencil; — a porcupine; — a kind of bird, Turdus salica.
s.f. A bar (of iron); an ingot (of gold or silver); a stake; a spit; a probe; — a continuous line, a streak.
more: n. m. S. शलाका 1. An iron bar. 2. An ingot (of gold or silver). 3. Streak; line. Sharāb kī salākh bandh gaī .
wety: From Sanskrit शलाका (śalākā).

def: v.t. To rule lines; to mark with lines, to streak.
s.m. = S شللي शलली śalalī, s.f. A porcupine's quill.
s.f. = S شلل शलल śalal, s.m. A porcupine's quill.

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be or become safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Safety, peace; salutation, greeting, compliments; parting salutation, adieu, farewell, good-bye
more: n. m. سلم was safe. 1. Blessing ( darūd ). 2. Salutation; compliments. Maiṅ to tum ko dūr hī se salām kartā hūṅ . I always salute you from a distance. (I keep my distance). 3. Adieu; farewell; goodbye. Aisī dostī ko salām hai! Adieu to such friendship! salām-alaikum, salām-alaik , Mah. n. f. lit . Peace be with you! Good morning. Mil gaë kī salām-alaik hai . Prov. Chance meetings and bowing acquaintances. salām k. , v. a. 1. To salute; greet; hail. 2. To bid adieu; say farewell. Jab se to sang nehā kīnā, sunā na sukh kā nām, Pītam terī pīt ko jhuk jhuk karūṅ salām! Since I did love you even in name no peace I've had with you, My husband! to such love as yours I humbly bid adieu. 3. To bow to or acknowledge one's superiority. Akelī jāo jo masjid meṅ tāq bharne ko Dugānā jān, tumheṅ jhuk-ke ham salām kareṅ! Jan Sāhab. salām phernā , Mah. v. n. To look to the right and then to the left afte…

(from سلم) s. m. 1. Salutation. 2. Peace, safety. سلام کرنا salām karnā, 1. To salute, to hail, to accost. 2. To give up, to desist, to refrain, to bid adieu. سلامٌ عليک salāmuṉ aﻌlaika, Peace or safety to thee: سلامٌ عليکم salāmuṉ aﻌlaikum (Heb. ) Peace, &c. to you (forms of salutation). سلام لينا salām lenā, To return the salutation.
subs: To look to the right and the left after prayer
(-ko), To bid adieu (to), to dismiss; (among Europeans) to present (one's) compliments (to); to request the presence (of a person); to desire permission to enter
Peace be with you; good morning to you
(-ko), To salute, greet, hail, accost; to bid adieu (to), say farewell (to); to give up, to desist or refrain (from); to bow (to), to acknowledge the superiority of
(-kā), To accept and return the salutation (of)
ex: Send my regards to your father.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَام (salām), itself from Arabic سَلَام (salām). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi سلام (slam).

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Saluting; submitting; cultivating peace; friendship; — peace, safety; — adj. Free from fault or flaw, sound; — sallamahuʼl-lāhǒ taʻālā, May the Most High God save or protect him.
more: (from سلم Heb. To be safe, entire or sound) 1. s. m. Peace, safety. 2. Saluting, submitting, cultivating peace, friendship. 3. adj. Free from fault or flaw. ¶ sullam, s. m. A stair. سلمه الله تعاليٰ sallamahu-l-lāhu taʼāﻌlạ̄, May the Almighty protect him.

r: ṣalʻam, ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, ṣallạ̄
def: a contrac. (often added to the name of Mohammad), formed from the abbreviation of the words ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, 'May God bless him and grant him peace' (see صلی ṣallạ̄).
more: A mysterious word (added often to the name of Maḥammad ) formed from the abbreviation of the following words, صلي الله عليه وسلم ṣallạ-l-lāh aﻌlaihi wa sallam, The blessing and peace of God be with him.

ety: for A. سلامة, inf. n. of سلم; see salām
def: s.f. Safety, salvation; tranquillity, peace, rest, repose; immunity; liberty; soundness; recovery; health; — adj. & adv. (used predicatively) Safe, sound, well; — in safety, safely, securely
more: adj. Safe; well. Khudā salāmat rakkhe! Benediction. Peace be with you! salāmat , adv. Safely; securely. 1. Salāmat pahuṅchā . 2. Nāk kuṭī mubārak; sir muṅḍā salāmat! Wom. Nose-cut, good fortune; head-shaven, hail! (A shameless woman.)
(from سلم) s. f. Safety, salvation, recovery, tranquillity, health; adjectively, Safe, well, in safety. سلامت رو salāmat-rau, An economist. سلامت روي salāmat-rau-ī, s. f. Economy, good management.
subs: s.m. An economist; a good manager or administrator; a well-conducted person
or rauʼī, s.f. Economy, good management; moderation; good behaviour
v.n. To be or remain safe and sound, to live long, to prosper.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَامَت (salāmat), from Arabic سَلَامَة (salāma).

def: s.f. Safety, security, protection; peace, tranquillity; health; good condition, well-being; the being alive and in health; — a kind of coarse cloth; — adv. In peace or safety; in health
more: n. f. 1. Protection; peace (امن 1.). Allah miyāṅ kī salāmtī paṛh-kar nyāz de do! Mah. Wom. 2. Health; good condition; life. Tumhārī salāmtī meṅ aisī bāteṅ hoṅgī. salāmtī se , Mah. Wom. intj. Heaven preserve! Uske to salamtī se do bachche hain . salāmtī kā jām pīnā , v. a. To drink to one's health.
s. f. 1. Health, safety; in health or safety. 2. A coarse kind of cloth.
subs: (-kī), To drink to the health (of), to quaff a cup to the health (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَامَتِی (salāmatī).

def: n. m. 1. An economist; a good manager or administrator. 2. A well-conducted person.
n. f. 1. Economy; good management; moderation. 2. Good behaviour. salāmat rahnā , v. n. To be safe and sound; to live long; prosper.

ety: salām+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Salutation; salute (as by presentation of arms, &c.), salute of cannon; — a present on being introduced to a superior; a present given to a landlord on granting a lease, &c.; a present (on particular occasions) to a person making a salute; a congratulatory present to a bridegroom (generally from the father-in-law's household; — cf. salām-karāʼī); — slope, incline; a pavement across the bed of a watercourse
more: n. f. 1. Salutation; presentation of arms; reception; welcome. Bādshāh kī salāmī ke vāste fauj khaṛī hai . 2. A salute of guns; salvo of artillery. Yeh topeṅ kisī Rājā kī salāmī meṅ dag rahī haīṅ . 3. A present to a superior, or to a bridegroom. It is given by the tenant to a landlord for granting him a lease of land, etc. or by one receiving an appointment, or paying a visit to a great man. 4. A slope ( ḍhalān ). Saṛak kī salāmī . 5. A pavement across the bed of a water-course; an Irish bridge. salāmī khānah-bārī , n. A compulsory present or tax exacted by the zamīndār from his tenant on his erecting a new hut.
s. f. 1. Salute. 2. A present given to a landlord for granting a lease, &c.
s. f. A present given on particular occasions to a person making a salute.
subs: s.f. A present from a farmer to his landlord to enable him to build his house; a compulsory present exacted by the landlord from a tenant on his erecting a new hut.

def: s.m. A curry of meat, fish, or vegetables.
more: n. m. H. salonā salted. Meat, fish, or vegetable curry.
s. m. Meat, or fish, &c. eaten with bread or rice.
s. m. Meat, fish, or vegetables dressed with condiments in various ways, a curry.
wety: Uncertain. Some theories: * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलेपन (*saṃlepana, “smearing together”), from सम्- (sam-) + लिप् (lip, “to smear”) + -न (-na). * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलापन (*saṃlāpana), from the root ली (lī, “to attach”). * Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀮𑁄𑀡 (saloṇa), 𑀲𑀮𑀽𑀡 (salūṇa), from Sanskrit सलवण (salavaṇa, “having salt”), from स- (sa-) + लवण (lavaṇa). Compare सलोना (salonā, “tasty”).

ety: sāl, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Yearly, annual; annually, per year; — s.m. Annual pension; annuity; — land taken up for the whole year
more: adj. Annual; yearly. sālānah , n. m. 1. Land taken up for the whole year. 2. Annual pension; an annuity. sālānahāmdanī , n. f. Annual income or revenue. sālānah hāsil, yā tahsīl . Yearly collections from a district or estate. sālānah hīn hayāt , n. A life-annuity. sālānah-dār , An annuitant; a pensioner. naqshajāt-i-sālānah . Annual returns.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سالانه (sālāna).

def: adj. Penetrable.
s.f. coutrac. of salūno, q.v.

ety: sāl, q.v.+ār = āwar = prep. ā+war = Zend ā+bara = S. आ+भर
def: s.m. Leader in war, general or commander-in-chief, a title given by Eastern princes to their nobles
more: n. m. In comp . A chief; head; sālār jaṅg , n. m. 1. A general or commander-in-chief. 2. ( Slang ) A brother-in-law. sālār-i-qāfla . The chief or leader of a caravan. sālār-i-qaum , n. m. The head of a clan or seet.
s. m. A chief, head, leader, general or commander (of an army), prince, captain. سالارقافله sālār-i-ḳāfila, The chief of a caravan, leader of a troop.
subs: s.m. Chief of a caravan, leader of a troop
s.m. Head of a clan or tribe.

def: s.m. A ladder; a flight of stairs or steps; — a bird-cage; — the claw of an elephant.
adj. (prop. fem. of s̤alās̤at), Three.

ety: for A. سلاسة, inf. n. of سلس 'to be easy,' &c.
def: s.f. Easiness, simplicity; plainness, clearness, perspicuity; facility; ductility; gentleness, tractableness; urbanity.
more: n. f. سلس was loose. 1. Simplicity. 2. Gentleness; urbanity. salāsat-i-zabān , n. f. Simplicity of language.
(from سلس) s. f. Perspicuity, clearness, plainness, facility, ductility, easiness, gentleness.

def: s.f. pl. (of silsilat), Chains.
s. m. (dakh.) Farrier ( Harris ): see سالوتري sālotarī.

ety: quasi-inf. n. of صلّي 'to pray,' ii of صلو
def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (esp. one of the five stated prayers enjoined on good Mohammadans in the course of the twenty-four hours); — invocation of (God's) blessing (on), a blessing, benediction; — the Fātiḥa or opening chapter of the Qorʼān.
more: (from صلا Ch. and To pray) s. f. 1. Prayer, benediction. 2. The first chapter of the ḳurʼān.

ety: quasi-inf. n. of صلّي 'to pray,' ii of صلو
def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (esp. one of the five stated prayers enjoined on good Mohammadans in the course of the twenty-four hours); — invocation of (God's) blessing (on), a blessing, benediction; — the Fātiḥa or opening chapter of the Qorʼān.

s.f. Prayer, &c. ( = صلات ṣalāt, q.v.); compassion or mercy (of God). s.f. Prayer, supplication (esp. one of the five stated prayers enjoined on good Mohammadans in the course of the twenty-four hours); — invocation of (God's) blessing (on), a blessing, benediction; — the Fātiḥa or opening chapter of the Qorʼān.
more: s. f. 1. Prayer, benediction. 2. Compassion or mercy of God.

def: s.m. pl. (of sult̤ān), Emperors, kings, monarchs.
more: n. Pl. of sultān , q. v. 1. Emperors; kings; princes. 2. The younger branches of a royal family; minor princes. Inheṅ bādshazādāh kaun kahtā hai, yeh to salātīnoṅ meṅ se haiṅ .
s. plur. of سلطان sult̤ān, q. v.

def: s.f. (ironic. & colloq.) Curses, maledictions; abuse, insult
more: n. f. 1. Blessings; benediction. 2. ( salvāteṅ ) Abuse; insult. salvāteṅ sunānā , v. a. To abuse roundly, or in set terms.

def: s.f. Burning; — crucifying, crucifixion; — teasing, fretting, vexing.
more: s. f. 1. (Ch. ) Crucifixion. 2. Burning. 3. Teasing, fretting, vexing. ¶ ṣulb, The loins; ( met. ) offspring.

ety: inf. n. of سلب 'to seize, carry off by force,' &c.
def: s.m. Seizing by force, spoiling, depriving of; deprivation; negation, denial, negative argument (in logic); — spoil, plunder, booty
more: adj. Wanting; non est. salb h. , v. n. To be wanting or lost. 1. Tāqat salb ho gai . 2. Tumhārī aql to salb ho gaī hai .
s. m. 1. A negative argument in logick. 2. Plunder. سلب کلي salb-i-kullī, General negation. سلب جزٴي salb-i-juz-ī, Special negation.
subs: v.n. To be carried off; to be deprived of; to be wanting in, be devoid of; to be lost.

def: s.f. Vice (in a horse).
s.m. Sage, Salvia.

adj. = salonā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Salt, salted; seasoned; tasteful, tasty, savoury; beautiful, handsome; interesting, piquant; intelligent; dark-complexioned, nut-brown (complexion); expressive (countenance).
more: adj. 1. Seasoned, tasteful. 2. Beautiful (when applied to a mistress).

ety: act. part. of صلح 'to be good, or just,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Good, right, just, righteous, virtuous, honest; sound, proper, fit, apt; — a good or virtuous man, a man of probity and honour; a sedate, steady person (opp. to enthusiast).
more: adj. صلح was good. Virtuous; righteous. javān-i-sāleh , n. m. A virtuous young man. Maiṅ pīr-i-gunāhgār, tum javān-i-sāleh . I am an old sinner, and you a virtuous youth.
(part. act. of صلح Heb. To be good or useful) adj. Good, apt, fit, proper. s. m. 1. A man of probity and honour. 2. A sedate, steady person (opposite to enthusiast).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَالِح (sālih), from Arabic صَالِح (ṣāliḥ).

def: (for A. صالحة, fem. of ṣāliḥ), adj. f. Good, virtuous, chaste, &c.
more: adj. f. (of صالح) Virtuous, chaste. (Plur. صالحات ṣālīḥāt ).

def: s.m. The phlegmatic humour, phlegm, rheum, defluxion, catarrh (syn. zukām).
n. f. A tall awkward woman or girl.
s.m. corr. of شلغم shalgam, q.v. s.m. A turnip (vulg. salgam).
(see سلگ) adj. (dakh.) Familiar, friendly (?): see هلا hilā.
s. (dakh.) Tameness ( Harris ).
plur. of سال sāl, Years, for years. سالـہ‍ا سال sālhā sāl, For years, many years, annually.
adv. For many years
subs: sālhāʼě-sāl, s.m. A course or series of years, several years; — adv. For years; year after year.

def: s.f. = salaij, q.v. s.f. Brother-in-law's wife ( = sarhaj).
s.m. = شالي śāli, q.v. adj. Annual, relating to the year; — s.f. Land taken by the year.
s.f. Wife's sister (esp. younger sister), sister-in-law.
adj. (f. -inī), Belonging to a house, domestic; — (as last member of compounds) endowed with, possessed of, having; inclined or disposed to; shining, resplendent with.
adj. Annual, relating to the year; — s.f. Land taken by the year.
more: n. f. S. श्याली Wife's sister. Sālī ādhī, nihālī sālhaj pūrī joë . Prov. Your sister-in-law your bedfellow by half, the consort of your brother-in-law wife whole.
s. f. Sister-in-law, wifeʼs sister, especially younger sister.

def: s.m. Rice; a kind of white rice (growing in deep water); red rice (requiring only a moist soil); — the civet-cat; pole-cat
subs: s.m. (f. -ī), lit. 'Receiving oblations of rice or corn'; a horse; name of a writer on veterinary subjects (cf. sālotrī, salotrī)

ety: v.n. fr. صلب 'to be hard'; 'to crucify'
def: adj. Hard, firm, rigid, stiff; hardy, strong; — three-cornered; cross-shaped; — s.m. A cross, crucifix
more: n. f. A cross or crucifix. salīb denā, salīb par chaṛhānā , v. a. To crucify.
(from صلب) 1. adj. Hard. 2. Three-cornered. 3. s. m. A crucifix, a cross.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَلِیب (salīb), borrowed from Arabic صَلِيب (ṣalīb), borrowed from Aramaic צְלִיבָא (ṣəlīḇā), borrowed from Akkadian 𒁇 (ṣalāpu, “to cross out, to pervert; to give difficulty or be crooked, not be easy or straight”).

ety: for A. سالبة, fem. of sālib, act. part. of سلب 'to seize'
def: s.m. A negative proposition (in logic).
more: n. m. ( Logic .) A negative proposition.
(part. act. of سلب To snatch) A negative sentence in logick.

def: s.m. See s.v. شالي śāli. adj. Annual, relating to the year; — s.f. Land taken by the year.

ety: act. part. of سلك 'to go along, to travel,' &c.
def: part. & s.m. Going, travelling; — traveller; (met.) devotee; a class of Moḥammadans who observe the law and lead a domestic life; an individual of that class (P. plur. sālikān).
more: (from سلک) 1. part. act. Going. 2. s. m. A traveller. 3. ( met. ) A devotee.

def: adj. Relating or belonging to the śāl tree; belonging to a hall, &c.; — s.m. A weaver; a village of artificers; — a toll, tax
subs: s.m. One of the main divisions of the Mahābhārat.

ety: for A. سليقة salīqat, v.n. fr. سلق 'to adapt or dispose to,' &c.
def: s.m. Nature, natural disposition or constitution; genius; taste; good disposition; method, knack, way; knowledge, skill, dexterity, address
more: n. m. See tamīz . Knack ( ḍhaṅg , 1). 1. Salīqah bāt kā yeh, kuchh zabāṅ jab apnī khole hai , To apne vālid-i-marhūm ko mahrūm bole hai . Sauda. His skill in language such that when to speak he opes his mouth, His father blessed in Heaven he calls his father damned in sooth. 2. Shart salīqa hai har kām meṅ . Prov. In every thing you do good judgment helps you through. salīqa-mand, salīqa-shiār, salīqah-dār, sāhib-i-salīqah, salīqa-vālā, salīqevālī , adj. See tamīz-dār . salīqa kī guftgū , n. m. Polite conversation. salīqa-i-majlis , n. m. Good breeding.

s. m. Nature, genius, taste, good disposition, method, knack, knowledge, dexterity, address. سليقه شعار salīḳa-shiʼāﻌr, adj. Of good disposition. سليقه گفتگو salīḳa-e-guft-gū, s. m. Polite conversation. صاحب سليقه ṣāḥib-salīḳa, A man of taste. سليقه مجلس salīḳa-e-majlis, s. m. Good breeding.
subs: salīqa-shěʻār, salīqa-mand, and salīqa-wālā, adj. Of good disposition; well-conducted; possessed of genius; discerning, discreet
s.m. or salīqe-kī guftgū, s.f. Polite conversation

def: n. m. The year of 13 months, which comes round every three years. sāl-girah ; H. baras-gāṅṭh , n. m. Birthday; the anniversary of a person's nativity when a knot is tied on a string or thread, kept as a record of his age. Hin. sāl-i-guzashtā ; Sant. dinkalom , The past year; last year. sāl-i-mālī , n. m. The revenue year. sāl-i-mahājnī . The mercantile year, concurrent with the Samvat year, but commencing two months later. sāl mohlat . Year of remission or grace. sāl-vār . By, or according to the year or years. sālvār vasūl bāqī . Accounts of the annual collections and balances for a series of years. sālhā sāl , adv. For years; year after year. ab-ke sāl, is sāl , n. The present or current year.
s.m. Water; liquid (in general).
adj. (f. -ā), Playful, sportive, wanton.

ety: act. part. of سلم 'to be safe,' &c.
def: adj. Safe, secure, free; sound, perfect; whole; (in Gram.) sound or regular (a verb or word, i.e. not having one of the weak letter ا, و, ي for one of its radical letters, or the second radical similar to the third).
more: adj. سلم was safe. 1. Safe. 2. Whole (ثابت 1.) 3. ( Gram .) Regular. baë-salam , See baë kāmil , under بيع
(part. act. of سلم Heb. To be safe or entire) adj. Safe, free, perfect, sound.

def: adj. Safe, secure, free; sound, perfect; healthy; right; pacific, mild, meek, affable
more: adj. سلم was sound. See pūrā , (3, 5). 1. Sound. 2. Healthy; right ( chaṅgā , 2.). 3. Mild; moderate; affable. salīm-ut-taba . Right-minded; sound. aql-i-salīm . Sound mind, sense, or reason.
(from سلم) adj. 1. Pacifick, mild, meek, affable. 2. Perfect, healthy. سليم الطبع salīmu-t̤-t̤abﻌ, adj. Affable, mild, gentle, pacifick; a real genius, a genuine poet.
subs: adj. & s.m. Right-minded; sound; affable, &c. ( = salīm)
a right-minded person; — a real genius

def: s.f. A kind of cloth.

ety: act. part. of ثلث 'to form a third,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Third; — a third person, a mediator, arbitrator, umpire
more: n. m. ثلث a third. A third person; referee; assessor; arbitrator; umpire ( bichaulyā ).
(from ثلث) s. m. 1. The third. 2. An umpire, an arbitrator, a mediator. ثالث ثلاثه s̤ālis̤ s̤alās̤a, s. m. Trinity. ثالث بالخير s̤ālis̤ bi-l-ḵẖair, An unprejudiced arbitrator.
subs: s.m. An unprejudiced or impartial arbitrator
s.m. An award by arbitration or by a jury, a deed of award.

ety: prob. corr. of A. salis; or an unclassical form fr. A. rt. سلس
def: adj. Easy, simple, plain, not abstruse
more: adj. سلس was loose. Simple; plain ( āsān 1). 1. Salīs ibārat . A simple style. 2. Salīs zabān . Simple language.
(prop. سلس salis from سلس) adj. Easy, not abstruse, simple, plain. سليس نويس salīs-navīs, Writing a plain hand.
subs: adj. & s.m. Writing a plain hand; — one who writes a plain hand.

ety: v.n. fr. سلس 'to be loose or slack,' &c.
def: adj. Easy, gentle, tractable, docile, mild, compliant, submissive; ductile.
more: (prop. salis ) adj. Easy, docile, gentle, mild, ductile.

ety: for A. ثالثة s̤ālis̤at, fem. of s̤ālis̤
def: adj. & s.f. Third; — mediatrix, arbitress.
more: s. f. An arbitress, a mediatrix.

def: An impartial or unprejudiced arbitrator.
s.f. Mediation, arbitration.
s.f. Easiness, simplicity, plainness, clearness.
n. m. An award by a jury; arbitration award.
n. m. Trinity ( taslīs ).
s.m. A canvas sack (in which baggage, &c. is fastened previously to being loaded on cattle).
s.m. A canvas sack (in which baggage, &c. is fastened previously to being loaded on cattle).
more: n. m. A canvass sack. Shalīte meṅ mekh, na lashkar meṅ Shekh . Prov. Tent pegs in pannier never keep, a Shekh in fighting force ne'er keep.
s. m. A canvass sack, &c. in which baggage is fastened previously to being loaded on cattle.

def: s.f. A river, stream.
more: s. f. A river: see سرتا.

def: s.m. corr. of shalīta, q.v.
s.m. Name of a celebrated sovereign of India, institutor of the era now called śāka or sākā (his capital was Pratishṭhāna on the Godāvarī: he was the enemy of Vikramāditya).
s.m. Name of a celebrated ancient sovereign of India, whose capital was Pratishṭhān on the Godāvarī (he was the institutor of the era now called śāka, and the enemy of Vikramāditya).
adj. &c. = sālāna, q.v. adj. & adv. Yearly, annual; annually, per year; — s.m. Annual pension; annuity; — land taken up for the whole year
more: (comp. of سال Year, and the adverbial particle آنه) 1. s. m. An annual stipend, annuity, salary. 2. adj. Annual. ساليانه دار sāliyāna-dār, An annuitant.

ety: inf. n. of سلخ 'to strip off,' &c.
def: s.f. The last or end (of a month), the last day (of a month of thirty days), the day of new moon.
more: (prop. salḵẖ, from سلخ He skinned, took off) s. f. The end of the month, or the day of the new moon: the last day of such a month as has thirty days.

def: s.f. The root of the lotus or water-lily.
more: s. f. The root of the Nymphæa lotus.

ety: inf. n. of سلّ 'to draw out or forth'
def: s.m. Drawing out or forth (esp. a sword from the scabbard).
3rd. pers. sing. pret. (of form ii of صلو), He blessed; or (optatively) may he bless
more: 3d pers. mas. sing. of past tense, He blessed or was merciful towards. صليٰ الله عليه ṣallạ-l-lāh aﻌlaihi, The blessing of God on him! صليٰ الله عليهم ṣallạ-l-lāh aﻌlaihim, The blessing of God be on them!
subs: (or -him), May God bless (or confer blessing upon) him (or them).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَلَّیٰ (sallā), from Arabic صَلَّى (ṣallā, “he blessed; may he bless”).

def: s.m. The plant Bignonia indica; — bark, rind.
s.m. A skinner, flayer.
more: (from سلخ To skin) s. m. A skinner.
wety: From Sanskrit शलाका (śalākā).

def: s.f. The gum olibanum tree, Boswellia thurifera.
s.f. A porcupine; — the gum olibanum tree, Boswellia thurifera
s.m. A kind of coarse cloth.
more: s. m. A kind of coarse cloth.
